description_long: This is a round-bodied ceramic vase with a narrow neck and two small, curved handles attached symmetrically on either side of the neck. The surface is predominantly a weathered off-white or light gray, with visible signs of age and patina, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements or handled frequently. Around the neck, there is a decorative band featuring a repeating geometric motif in a dark gray or black color, and a triangular pattern with lines and dots is present on the upper body. The handles are robust and slightly thick, blending smoothly into the body and neck. The mouth of the vase is wide and flat, suitable for holding flowers or other decorative items. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, with subtle ornamental details that hint at traditional or historical craftsmanship.
description: A weathered, round-bodied ceramic vase with two handles and geometric dark motifs around the neck and upper body. The surface is aged, matte, and off-white with visible patina.
description_view_0: Front view showing both handles, the geometric neck band, and the triangular motif on the upper body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one handle in profile and the vase's rounded body, with less visible decoration.
description_view_2: Opposite front view, similar to view 0, showing both handles and the main decorative motifs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the other handle in profile and the plain, rounded body.
